generatedSentence: Elias, an eccentric older man, resided in a secluded enclosure on a volcanic island, where the morning dewdrop clung to the vibrant myrtle. His ambition was to redesign the entire coastline, an audacious plan to offset the industrial vehicles constantly passing by. He envisioned a towering, unsinkable structure, its surface painted a radiant vermilion, designed to expand into a public art space. He would often chuckle to himself, knowing whoever visited would be met with profound beauty. His workshop, a marvel of electrical wiring and intricate lacquerware, was a testament to his unwavering focus. He meticulously planned every knot in the colossal frame, refusing to make an assumption that anything less than perfection would suffice. He aimed to furnish the entire area with new life, a project so grand it would make the famed critic Donner rethink his entire career. He even considered adding whimsical mechanical clowns to the entrance. His vision, however, attracted unwanted attention. A sinister imperial conglomerate, notorious for its brazen highjack of local resources, had a definite interest in his private island. Their legal team, led by a wrinkly but sharp attorney, deployed a sophisticated network of legal maneuvers, trying to unhook Elias from his property. He felt their corporate omnipotence pressing down, a heavy kettle of grievances bubbling. One day, a drone, barely a dandelion seed in the distance, hovered over his workshop, its tiny microphone capturing every sound. Elias, unapologetic in his defiance, simply farted loudly in its direction, a gesture of his contempt. He saw their threats as mere nothings, their attempts to marzipan his dream into their corporate image. His old computer, hooked up to a faulty soundbar, often buzzed. He kept his grandfather's old moneybox full of copper coins under his bed. The corporate giants assumed he'd easily give up his vertical garden and his beloved pet tortoise, but they had underestimated his stubbornness. He felt like a lone goat bleating a frustrated hallelujah against the corporate machine. The urgency escalated. Elias, refusing to be overpowered, knew he needed to make a stand. He had acquired a vast knowledge of local defenses, willed into existence from years of observation, and a strange variety of ancient weaponry. He donned his old, oily leather apron, looking less like a gardener and more like a ghoul preparing for battle. The corporation's agents, a feckless bunch, were already infiltrating the neighborhood, their intentions unclear. Elias had meticulously packed his tools and supplies, every bolt and wire in its place, his resolve ready. He'd even polished the antique jewelry his grandmother left him, seeing its beautiful gleam in the twilight. From his hidden, almost monastery-like bunker, he watched them approach. The agents, considering his defenses overrated, expected an easy victory. Little did they know, Elias had a newborn device, still sticky with fresh inking, ready to unleash an electromagnetic pulse. He had not shirked his duty to protect his traditional way of life. They expected to encounter a fantastical manticore, but instead found a man ready to weave a new fate. With a toothy grin, Elias activated his device, ejecting a powerful pulse that disabled the corporate agents' equipment. They retreated, their efforts to increase their hijackings of his land thwarted, their greed an exposed parasite. Elias knew his victory was a noble act, a prayer answered not by a divine officiant, but by his own willed determination. He watched the last wisps of smoke disappear into the night sky, their attempts to claim his land left unpunished by legal means, but defeated by ingenuity. He celebrated, gladly sharing a bottle of vodka and some cranberry juice with his old pet tortoise. His community, a silent brotherhood who had watched his struggles, started an excavation project, expanding his defenses farther. He finally felt at peace, no longer needing to wield knives or jackknives in self-defense. The threat of atrophy to his artistic spirit had passed. He even considered adding a waterslide to his evolving art installation, a whimsical nod to his renewed freedom, and ate some fresh kiwis. His bio would now read: "Artist, Inventor, and Corporation Tamer." He even bought a bongo drum to celebrate, his own private army of ingenuity.
panelSentences: Elias, an eccentric older man, resided in a secluded enclosure on a volcanic island, where the morning dewdrop clung to the vibrant myrtle. His ambition was to redesign the entire coastline, an audacious plan to offset the industrial vehicles constantly passing by. He envisioned a towering, unsinkable structure, its surface painted a radiant vermilion, designed to expand into a public art space. He would often chuckle to himself, knowing whoever visited would be met with profound beauty. His workshop, a marvel of electrical wiring and intricate lacquerware, was a testament to his unwavering focus. He meticulously planned every knot in the colossal frame, refusing to make an assumption that anything less than perfection would suffice. He aimed to furnish the entire area with new life, a project so grand it would make the famed critic Donner rethink his entire career. He even considered adding whimsical mechanical clowns to the entrance.

His vision, however, attracted unwanted attention. A sinister imperial conglomerate, notorious for its brazen highjack of local resources, had a definite interest in his private island. Their legal team, led by a wrinkly but sharp attorney, deployed a sophisticated network of legal maneuvers, trying to unhook Elias from his property. He felt their corporate omnipotence pressing down, a heavy kettle of grievances bubbling. One day, a drone, barely a dandelion seed in the distance, hovered over his workshop, its tiny microphone capturing every sound. Elias, unapologetic in his defiance, simply farted loudly in its direction, a gesture of his contempt. He saw their threats as mere nothings, their attempts to marzipan his dream into their corporate image. His old computer, hooked up to a faulty soundbar, often buzzed. He kept his grandfather's old moneybox full of copper coins under his bed. The corporate giants assumed he'd easily give up his vertical garden and his beloved pet tortoise, but they had underestimated his stubbornness. He felt like a lone goat bleating a frustrated hallelujah against the corporate machine.

The urgency escalated. Elias, refusing to be overpowered, knew he needed to make a stand. He had acquired a vast knowledge of local defenses, willed into existence from years of observation, and a strange variety of ancient weaponry. He donned his old, oily leather apron, looking less like a gardener and more like a ghoul preparing for battle. The corporation's agents, a feckless bunch, were already infiltrating the neighborhood, their intentions unclear. Elias had meticulously packed his tools and supplies, every bolt and wire in its place, his resolve ready. He'd even polished the antique jewelry his grandmother left him, seeing its beautiful gleam in the twilight. From his hidden, almost monastery-like bunker, he watched them approach. The agents, considering his defenses overrated, expected an easy victory. Little did they know, Elias had a newborn device, still sticky with fresh inking, ready to unleash an electromagnetic pulse. He had not shirked his duty to protect his traditional way of life. They expected to encounter a fantastical manticore, but instead found a man ready to weave a new fate.

With a toothy grin, Elias activated his device, ejecting a powerful pulse that disabled the corporate agents' equipment. They retreated, their efforts to increase their hijackings of his land thwarted, their greed an exposed parasite. Elias knew his victory was a noble act, a prayer answered not by a divine officiant, but by his own willed determination. He watched the last wisps of smoke disappear into the night sky, their attempts to claim his land left unpunished by legal means, but defeated by ingenuity. He celebrated, gladly sharing a bottle of vodka and some cranberry juice with his old pet tortoise. His community, a silent brotherhood who had watched his struggles, started an excavation project, expanding his defenses farther. He finally felt at peace, no longer needing to wield knives or jackknives in self-defense. The threat of atrophy to his artistic spirit had passed. He even considered adding a waterslide to his evolving art installation, a whimsical nod to his renewed freedom, and ate some fresh kiwis. His bio would now read: "Artist, Inventor, and Corporation Tamer." He even bought a bongo drum to celebrate, his own private army of ingenuity.
rawPanelSentences: Elias, an **eccentric** older man, resided in a secluded **enclosure** on a volcanic **island**, where the morning **dewdrop** clung to the vibrant **myrtle**. His ambition was to **redesign** the entire coastline, an audacious plan to **offset** the industrial **vehicles** constantly passing by. He envisioned a towering, **unsinkable** structure, its surface painted a radiant **vermilion**, designed to **expand** into a public art space. He would often **chuckle** to himself, knowing **whoever** visited would be met with profound **beauty**. His workshop, a marvel of **electrical** wiring and intricate **lacquerware**, was a testament to his unwavering **focus**. He meticulously planned every **knot** in the colossal frame, refusing to make an **assumption** that **anything** less than perfection would suffice. He aimed to **furnish** the entire area with new life, a project so grand it would make the famed critic **Donner** rethink his entire career. He even considered adding whimsical mechanical **clowns** to the entrance.

His vision, however, attracted unwanted attention. A sinister **imperial** conglomerate, notorious for its brazen **highjack** of local resources, had a **definite** interest in his **private** island. Their legal team, led by a **wrinkly** but sharp attorney, deployed a sophisticated **network** of legal maneuvers, trying to **unhook** Elias from his property. He felt their corporate **omnipotence** pressing down, a heavy **kettle** of grievances bubbling. One day, a drone, barely a **dandelion** seed in the **distance**, hovered over his workshop, its tiny **microphone** capturing every sound. Elias, **unapologetic** in his defiance, simply **farted** loudly in its direction, a gesture of his contempt. He saw their threats as mere **nothings**, their attempts to **marzipan** his dream into their corporate image. His old **computer**, hooked up to a faulty **soundbar**, often buzzed. He kept his grandfather's old **moneybox** full of **copper** coins **under** his bed. The corporate giants assumed he'd easily give up his **vertical** garden and his beloved pet **tortoise**, but they had underestimated his stubbornness. He felt like a lone **goat** bleating a frustrated **hallelujah** against the corporate machine.

The **urgency** escalated. Elias, refusing to be **overpower**ed, knew he needed to make a stand. He had acquired a vast **knowledge** of local defenses, **willed** into existence from years of observation, and a strange **variety** of ancient weaponry. He donned his old, **oily** leather apron, looking less like a **gardener** and more like a **ghoul** preparing for battle. The corporation's agents, a **feckless** bunch, were already infiltrating the **neighborhood**, their intentions **unclear**. Elias had meticulously **packed** his tools and supplies, every **bolt** and wire in its place, his resolve **ready**. He'd even polished the antique **jewelry** his grandmother left him, seeing its **beautiful** gleam in the twilight. From his hidden, almost **monastery**-like bunker, he watched them approach. The agents, considering his defenses **overrated**, expected an easy victory. Little did they know, Elias had a **newborn** device, still sticky with fresh **inking**, ready to unleash an electromagnetic pulse. He had not **shirked** his duty to protect his **traditional** way of life. They expected to encounter a fantastical **manticore**, but instead found a man ready to **weave** a new fate.

With a **toothy** grin, Elias activated his device, **ejecting** a powerful pulse that disabled the corporate agents' equipment. They retreated, their efforts to **increase** their **hijackings** of his land thwarted, their greed an exposed **parasite**. Elias knew his victory was a **noble** act, a **prayer** answered not by a divine **officiant**, but by his own **willed** determination. He watched the last **wisps** of smoke disappear into the night sky, their attempts to claim his land left **unpunished** by legal means, but defeated by ingenuity. He celebrated, **gladly** sharing a bottle of **vodka** and some **cranberry** juice with his old pet **tortoise**. His community, a silent **brotherhood** who had watched his struggles, started an **excavation** project, expanding his defenses **farther**. He finally felt at peace, no longer needing to wield **knives** or **jackknives** in self-defense. The threat of **atrophy** to his artistic spirit had passed. He even considered adding a **waterslide** to his evolving art installation, a whimsical nod to his renewed freedom, and ate some fresh **kiwis**. His **bio** would now read: "Artist, Inventor, and Corporation **Tamer**." He even bought a **bongo** drum to celebrate, his own private **army** of ingenuity.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant indie comic style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Character portrait of Elias: an elderly man with a long grey beard, a weathered face full of wrinkles, wearing a greasy leather apron, and a knowing, toothy grin. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elias, a bearded, wrinkly man, meticulously wiring a complex, colorful art installation in his cluttered island workshop, surrounded by exotic lacquerware, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elias, the elderly man, defiantly farting towards a small drone hovering outside his workshop window, his pet tortoise looking up amusedly, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elias, the mature man, activating a glowing, handheld device, causing a group of corporate agents with stern, adult faces to stumble and retreat in disarray from his property, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elias, the old man, sitting relaxed, happily sharing a bottle of vodka with his tortoise on a cliff overlooking the ocean, a whimsical waterslide partially visible in the background, long shot. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue, NO children.

definitions.definition: Anything that acts as counterbalance; a compensating equivalent.
definitions.example: Today's victory was an offset to yesterday's defeat.

definitions.definition: A form of countertrade arrangement, in which the seller agrees to purchase within a set time frame products of a certain value from the buying country. This kind of agreement may be used in large international public sector contracts such as arms sales.
(c. 1555) A time at which something begins; outset.
The offset printing process, in which ink is carried from a metal plate to a rubber blanket and from there to the printing surface.
definitions.example: offset lithographs

definitions.definition: The difference between a target memory address and a base address.
definitions.example: An array of bytes uses its index as the offset, of words a multiple thereof.

definitions.definition: (signal analysis) The displacement between the base level of a measurement and the signal's real base level.
definitions.example: The raw signal data was subjected to a baseline correction process to subtract the sensor's offset and drift variations.

definitions.definition: The distance by which one thing is out of alignment with another.
definitions.example: There is a small offset between the switch and the indicator which some users found confusing.

definitions.definition: A short distance measured at right angles from a line actually run to some point in an irregular boundary, or to some object.
An abrupt bend in an object, such as a rod, by which one part is turned aside out of line, but nearly parallel, with the rest; the part thus bent aside.
A short prostrate shoot that takes root and produces a tuft of leaves, etc.
A spur from a range of hills or mountains.
A horizontal ledge on the face of a wall, formed by a diminution of its thickness, or by the weathering or upper surface of a part built out from it; a set-off.
A terrace on a hillside.
To compensate for, by applying a change in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: I'll offset the time difference locally.

definitions.definition: To form an offset in (a wall, rod, pipe, etc.).
A type or way of weaving.
definitions.example: That rug has a very tight weave.

definitions.definition: Human or artificial hair worn to alter one's appearance, either to supplement or to cover the natural hair.
To form something by passing lengths or strands of material over and under one another.
definitions.example: This loom weaves yarn into sweaters.

definitions.definition: To spin a cocoon or a web.
definitions.example: Spiders weave beautiful but deadly webs.

definitions.definition: To unite by close connection or intermixture.
To compose creatively and intricately; to fabricate.
definitions.example: to weave the plot of a story

definitions.definition: A droplet of water formed as dew.
(1800s) A slow pitch.
A contiguous area of land, smaller than a continent, totally surrounded by water.
An entity surrounded by other entities that are very different from itself.
definitions.example: an island of colors on a butterfly's wing

definitions.definition: A superstructure on an aircraft carrier's deck.
A traffic island.
definitions.example: the island in the middle of a roundabout

definitions.definition: An unincorporated area wholly surrounded by one or more incorporated areas.
(grammar) A phrase from which a wh-word cannot be extracted without yielding invalid grammar.
To surround with water; make into an island.
To set, dot (as if) with islands.
To isolate.
A vivid red synthetic pigment made of mercury sulfide.
A bright orange-red colour.
A type of red dye worn in the parting of the hair by married Hindu women.
The red skin of the lips or its border with the skin of the face.
The kermes or cochineal insect.
The cochineal dye made from this insect.
To color or paint vermilion.
Having a brilliant red colour.
Having the color of the vermilion dye.
A conveyance; a device for carrying or transporting substances, objects or individuals.
A medium for expression of talent or views.
A liquid content (e.g. oil) which acts as a binding and drying agent in paint. (FM 55-501).
(pharmaceuticals) The main excipient (such as an oil or gel) that conveys the active ingredient of a drug.
An entity to achieve an end.
A mode or method of spiritual practice; a yana.
An animal or (rarely) a plant on which a Hindu deity rides or sits
A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
definitions.example: Climbers must make sure that all knots are both secure and of types that will not weaken the rope.

definitions.definition: (of hair, etc) A tangled clump.
definitions.example: The nurse was brushing knots from the protesting child's hair.

definitions.definition: A maze-like pattern.
A non-self-intersecting closed curve in (e.g., three-dimensional) space that is an abstraction of a knot (in sense 1 above).
definitions.example: A knot can be defined as a non-self-intersecting broken line whose endpoints coincide: when such a knot is constrained to lie in a plane, then it is simply a polygon.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation.
definitions.example: I got into a knot when I inadvertently insulted a policeman.

definitions.definition: The whorl left in lumber by the base of a branch growing out of the tree's trunk.
definitions.example: When preparing to tell stories at a campfire, I like to set aside a pile of pine logs with lots of knots, since they burn brighter and make dramatic pops and cracks.

definitions.definition: Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
definitions.example: Jeremy had a knot on his head where he had bumped it on the bedframe.

definitions.definition: A protuberant joint in a plant.
Any knob, lump, swelling, or protuberance.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis in members of the dog family, Canidae
The point on which the action of a story depends; the gist of a matter.
definitions.example: the knot of the tale

definitions.definition: A node.
A kind of epaulet; a shoulder knot.
A group of people or things.
A bond of union; a connection; a tie.

A unit of speed, equal to one nautical mile per hour. (From the practice of counting the number of knots in the log-line (as it is paid out) in a standard time. Traditionally spaced at one every 1/120 of a mile.)
definitions.example: Cedric claimed his old yacht could make 12 knots.

definitions.definition: A nautical mile
The bulbus glandis
In omegaverse fiction, a bulbus glandis-like structure on the penis of a male alpha, which ties him to an omega during intercourse.
To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.
definitions.example: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.

definitions.definition: To form wrinkles in the forehead, as a sign of concentration, concern, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: She knotted her brow in concentration while attempting to unravel the tangled strands.

definitions.definition: To unite closely; to knit together.
To entangle or perplex; to puzzle.
To form knots.
To knit knots for a fringe.
One who does not behave like others.
A kook; a person of bizarre habits or beliefs.
A circle not having the same centre as another.
A disk or wheel with its axis off centre, giving a reciprocating motion.
Not at or in the centre; away from the centre.
Not perfectly circular; elliptical.
definitions.example: As of 2008, Margaret had the most eccentric orbit of any moon in the solar system, though Nereid's mean eccentricity is greater.

definitions.definition: Having a different center; not concentric.
(of a person) Deviating from the norm; behaving unexpectedly or differently; unconventional and slightly strange.

(of a motion) Against or in the opposite direction of contraction of a muscle (e.g., such as results from flexion of the lower arm (bending of the elbow joint) by an external force while contracting the triceps and other elbow extensor muscles to control that movement; opening of the jaw while flexing the masseter).
Having different goals or motives.
Something enclosed, i.e. inserted into a letter or similar package.
definitions.example: There was an enclosure with the letter — a photo.

definitions.definition: The act of enclosing, i.e. the insertion or inclusion of an item in a letter or package.
definitions.example: The enclosure of a photo with your letter is appreciated.

definitions.definition: An area, domain, or amount of something partially or entirely enclosed by barriers.
definitions.example: He faced punishment for creating the fenced enclosure in a public park.

definitions.definition: The act of separating and surrounding an area, domain, or amount of something with a barrier.
definitions.example: At first, untrained horses resist enclosure.

definitions.definition: (British History) The post-feudal process of subdivision of common lands for individual ownership.
definitions.example: Strip-farming disappeared after enclosure.

definitions.definition: The area of a convent, monastery, etc where access is restricted to community members.
An electrical engineer.
Related to electricity (or electronics)
To change (something) from a smaller form and/or size to a larger one; to spread out or lay open.
definitions.example: You can expand this compact umbrella to cover a large table.

definitions.definition: To increase the extent, number, volume or scope of (something).
definitions.example: A flower expands its leaves.

definitions.definition: To express (something) at length and/or in detail.
To rewrite (an expression) as a longer, yet equivalent sum of terms.
definitions.example: Use the binomial theorem to expand {(x+1)}^4.

definitions.definition: (of an expression) To become, by rewriting, a longer, yet equivalent sum of terms.
definitions.example: The expression {(x+1)}^4 expands to x^4 + 4x^3 + 6x^2 + 4x + 1.

definitions.definition: To multiply both the numerator and the denominator of a fraction by the same natural number yielding a fraction of equal value
To change from a smaller form/size to a larger one.
definitions.example: Many materials expand when heated.

definitions.definition: To increase in extent, number, volume or scope.
To speak or write at length or in detail.
definitions.example: He expanded on his plans for the business.

definitions.definition: To feel generous or optimistic.
The quality of being (especially visually) attractive, pleasing, fine or good-looking; comeliness.
definitions.example: 1988, "… beauty and recollection, like danger, glamour, greed, hunger- everything but disappointment and desire- were concepts belonging to other people.” -Second Son, Robert Ferro

definitions.definition: Someone who is beautiful.
definitions.example: Brigitte Bardot was a renowned beauty.

definitions.definition: Something that is particularly good or pleasing.
definitions.example: What a goal! That was a real beauty!

definitions.definition: An excellent or egregious example of something.
(with the definite article) The excellence or genius of a scheme or decision.
definitions.example: The beauty of the deal is it costs nothing!

definitions.definition: A beauty quark (now called bottom quark).
Beauty treatment; cosmetology.
Prevailing style or taste; rage; fashion.
(in the plural) Beautiful passages or extracts of poetry.
To make beautiful.
Of high quality, well done.
definitions.example: He made a beauty pass through the neutral zone.

definitions.definition: Thanks!
Cool!
definitions.example: It's the long weekend. Beauty!

definitions.definition: A substance that is applied as a liquid or paste, and dries into a solid coating that protects or adds color/colour to an object or surface to which it has been applied.
(in the plural) A set of containers or blocks of paint of different colors/colours, used for painting pictures.
The free-throw lane, construed with the.
definitions.example: The Nimrods are strong on the outside, but not very good in the paint.

definitions.definition: Paintballs.
definitions.example: I am running low on paint for my marker.

definitions.definition: A face card (king, queen, or jack).
Graphics drawn using an input device, not scanned or generated.
Makeup.
Any substance fixed with latex to harden it.
To apply paint to.
To apply in the manner that paint is applied.
To cover (something) with spots of colour, like paint.
To create (an image) with paints.
definitions.example: to paint a portrait or a landscape

definitions.definition: To practise the art of painting pictures.
definitions.example: I've been painting since I was a young child.

definitions.definition: To draw an element in a graphical user interface.
To depict or portray.
definitions.example: She sued the author of the biography, claiming it painted her as a duplicitous fraud.

definitions.definition: To color one's face by way of beautifying it.
To direct a radar beam toward.
definitions.example: "We'll paint the target for the flyboys," the JTAC said.

definitions.definition: A plan for making changes to the structure and functions of an artifact, building or system so as to better serve the purpose of the original design, or to serve purposes different from those set forth in the original design.
To lay out or plan a new version of something previously laid out or planned.
Of a ship: that cannot be sunk.
definitions.example: The Titanic was said to be unsinkable.

definitions.definition: That cannot be overcome or defeated.
definitions.example: unsinkable optimism

definitions.definition: An evergreen shrub or small tree of the genus Myrtus, native to southern Europe and north Africa.
A slapstick performance artist often associated with a circus and usually characterized by bright, oversized clothing, a red nose, face paint, and a brightly colored wig.
A person who acts in a silly fashion.
A stupid person.
A man of coarse nature and manners; an awkward fellow; an illbred person; a boor.
One who works upon the soil; a rustic; a churl; a yokel.
To act in a silly or playful fashion.
To ridicule.
A quiet laugh.
To laugh quietly or inwardly.
To communicate through chuckling.
definitions.example: She chuckled her assent to my offer as she got in the car.

definitions.definition: To make the sound of a chicken; to cluck.
To call together, or call to follow, as a hen calls her chickens; to cluck.
To fondle; to indulge or pamper.
Someone or something of importance.
Any object, act, state, event, or fact whatever; a thing of any kind; something or other.
definitions.example: I would not do it for anything.

definitions.definition: (with “as” or “like”) Expressing an indefinite comparison.
The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.
definitions.example: His assumption of secretarial duties was timely.

definitions.definition: The act of taking for granted, or supposing a thing without proof; a supposition; an unwarrantable claim.
definitions.example: Their assumption of his guilt disqualified them from jury duty.

definitions.definition: The thing supposed; a postulate, or proposition assumed; a supposition.
The minor or second proposition in a categorical syllogism.
The taking of a person up into heaven.
A festival in honor of the ascent of the Virgin Mary into heaven, celebrated on 15 August.
Assumptio.
Material used to create an engineered product.
To provide a place with furniture, or other equipment.
To supply or give (something).
To supply (somebody) with something.
A point at which reflected or refracted rays of light converge.
definitions.example: The heat of sunlight at the focus of a magnifying glass can easily set dry leaves on fire.

definitions.definition: A point of a conic at which rays reflected from a curve or surface converge.
The fact of the convergence of light on the photographic medium.
definitions.example: Unfortunately, the license plate is out of focus in this image.

definitions.definition: The quality of the convergence of light on the photographic medium.
definitions.example: During this scene, the boy’s face shifts subtly from soft focus into sharp focus.

definitions.definition: Concentration of attention.
definitions.example: I believe I can bring the high degree of focus required for this important job.

definitions.definition: The exact point of where an earthquake occurs, in three dimensions (underneath the epicentre).
definitions.example: The earthquake's focus was at exactly 37 degrees north, 18 degrees south, seventy five meters below the ground.

definitions.definition: The indicator of the currently active element in a user interface.
definitions.example: Text entered at the keyboard or pasted from a clipboard is sent to the component which currently has the focus.

definitions.definition: The most important word or phrase in a sentence or passage, or the one that imparts information.
An object used in casting a magic spell.
(followed by on or upon) To concentrate one's attention.
definitions.example: I have to focus on my work.

definitions.definition: To cause (rays of light, etc) to converge at a single point.
To adjust (a lens, an optical instrument) in order to position an image with respect to the focal plane.
definitions.example: You'll need to focus the microscope carefully in order to capture the full detail of this surface.

definitions.definition: To concentrate one’s attention.
definitions.example: If you're going to beat your competitors, you need to focus.

definitions.definition: To transfer the input focus to (a visual element), so that it receives subsequent input.
definitions.example: The text box won't receive the user's keystrokes unless you explicitly focus it.

definitions.definition: Decorative items coated with lacquer.
(interrogative) Who ever: an emphatic form of who.
definitions.example: Whoever thought up that stupid idea?

definitions.definition: (fused relative) Any person or persons that.
definitions.example: Whoever breaks the law will be punished.

definitions.definition: (fused relative) The person that (no matter who).
definitions.example: I don't know what it is. Ask whoever put it there.

definitions.definition: Regardless of the person or persons that.

definitions.definition: To beat up; clobber; thrash.

Relating to the British imperial system of measurement.
Very grand or fine.

Preflop, the position two before the dealer.

definitions.definition: Belonging to, concerning, or accessible only to an individual person or a specific group.

definitions.definition: Not in governmental office or employment.

definitions.definition: Not publicly known; not open; secret.

definitions.definition: Secretive; reserved.

definitions.definition: A person employed to perform computations; one who computes.
(by restriction) A male computer, where the female computer is called a computress.

A programmable electronic device that performs mathematical calculations and logical operations, especially one that can process, store and retrieve large amounts of data very quickly; now especially, a small one for personal or home use employed for manipulating text or graphics, accessing the Internet, or playing games or media.
A reddish-brown, malleable, ductile metallic element with high electrical and thermal conductivity, symbol Cu, and atomic number 29.
The reddish-brown colour/color of copper.

Made of copper.
Having the reddish-brown colour/color of copper.
(impolite) To emit digestive gases from the anus; to flatulate.

To emit (fumes, gases, etc.).
(grammar) A word or phrase that designates a specified or identified person or entity.
Anything that is defined or determined.
Having distinct limits.

definitions.definition: Free from any doubt.

definitions.definition: Determined; resolved; decided.
Designating an identified or immediately identifiable person or thing, or group of persons or things

Of or pertaining to vertical markets.

Multiple computers and other devices connected together to share information

To unfasten by means of hooks.

Not apologetic, especially when being apologetic would be appropriate.

Any of various land-dwelling reptiles, of the family Testudinidae or the order Testudines , whose body is enclosed in a shell (carapace plus plastron). The animal can withdraw its head and four legs partially into the shell, providing some protection from predators.
An old person.
Having wrinkles.
Any of the several species of plant in the genus Taraxacum, characterised by yellow flower heads and notched, broad-ended leaves, especially the common dandelion (Taraxacum officinale).

A yellow colour, like that of the flower.
Of a yellow colour, like that of the flower.
The amount of space between two points, usually geographical points, usually (but not necessarily) measured along a straight line.

One who tames or subdues.
Having prominent teeth
A clear distilled alcoholic liquor made from grain mash.
A serving of the above beverage.
Neutral spirits distilled (or treated after distillation) so as to have no distinctive character, aroma, taste or color.

A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.
A weapon designed with the aforementioned specifications intended for slashing and/or stabbing and too short to be called a sword. A dagger.
Any blade-like part in a tool or a machine designed for cutting, such as that of a chipper.
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
A striped bovine mammal found in Africa, Tragelaphus eurycerus.
Biographical sketch
Bioscope; cinema
Biological.
definitions.example: a bio detergent

definitions.definition: A person's life story, especially one published.
definitions.example: There are many biographies of Benjamin Franklin.

definitions.definition: The art of writing this kind of story.
The study of all life or living matter.
The living organisms of a particular region.
The structure, function, and behavior of an organism or type of organism.
definitions.example: the biology of the whale

definitions.definition: Not punished
A demand of ownership made for something.
definitions.example: a claim of ownership

definitions.definition: The thing claimed.
The right or ground of demanding.
definitions.example: You don't have any claim on my time, since I'm no longer your employee.

definitions.definition: A new statement of something one believes to be the truth, usually when the statement has yet to be verified or without valid evidence provided.
definitions.example: The company's share price dropped amid claims of accounting fraud.

definitions.definition: A demand of ownership for previously unowned land.
definitions.example: Miners had to stake their claims during the gold rush.

definitions.definition: A legal demand for compensation or damages.
To demand ownership of.
To state a new fact, typically without providing evidence to prove it is true.
To demand ownership or right to use for land.
To demand compensation or damages through the courts.
To be entitled to anything; to deduce a right or title; to have a claim.
To cause the loss of, usually by violent means.
definitions.example: A fire claimed two homes.

definitions.definition: To proclaim.
To call or name.
A compact folding knife.
definitions.example: He kept a jack-knife in his pocket for various tasks.

definitions.definition: The front-dive pike, in which the body folds and unfolds.
definitions.example: It took me hundreds of dives to master even the simple jackknife

definitions.definition: A semi-trailer truck accident in which the vehicle mimics the closing of a jack-knife.
definitions.example: I have seen several jack-knives along that dangerous stretch of road.

definitions.definition: A resampling method that applies estimators to all subsamples that each omit a single different group (possibly of a single datapoint) of the original sample to provide a sample distribution of the estimate.
To help forward; to assist.
To encourage growth; to support progress or growth of something; to promote.
definitions.example: Further the economy.

definitions.definition: A recreational slide with water flowing down it to reduce friction, sometimes involving the participant sitting on a mat or raft and sometimes leading into a pool at the bottom.
An amount by which a quantity is increased.
For a quantity, the act or process of becoming larger
Offspring, progeny
The creation of one or more new stitches; see Increase (knitting).
(of a quantity, etc.) To become larger or greater.
definitions.example: His rage only increased when I told him of the lost money.

definitions.definition: To make (a quantity, etc.) larger.
To multiply by the production of young; to be fertile, fruitful, or prolific.
To become more nearly full; to show more of the surface; to wax.
definitions.example: The Moon increases.

definitions.definition: The act of one who hijacks; the seizure of vehicles.
The instance of such an act; the seizure of a vehicle.
